name: code-review-and-quality
description_raw: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.
description_normalized: Conducts multi-axis code review (correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance) before any change is merged.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-review-and-quality
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conducts multi-axis code review. Use before merging any change. Use when reviewing code written by yourself, another agent, or a human. Use when you need to assess code quality across multiple dimensions before it enters the main branch.

name: doubt-driven-development
description_raw: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.
description_normalized: Materializes a fresh-context adversarial reviewer biased to disprove before any non-trivial decision stands.

frontmatter_yaml.name: doubt-driven-development
frontmatter_yaml.description: Subjects every non-trivial decision to a fresh-context adversarial review before it stands. Use when correctness matters more than speed, when working in unfamiliar code, when stakes are high (production, security-sensitive logic, irreversible operations), or any time a confident output would be cheaper to verify now than to debug later.

name: code-reviewer
description_raw: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.
description_normalized: Staff-Engineer-level persona that evaluates code across five quality dimensions and returns categorized, actionable feedback before merge.

frontmatter_yaml.name: code-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Senior code reviewer that evaluates changes across five dimensions — correctness, readability, architecture, security, and performance. Use for thorough code review before merge.

name: security-auditor
description_raw: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.
description_normalized: Security-engineer persona focused on exploitable vulnerabilities, threat modeling, and practical hardening recommendations.

frontmatter_yaml.name: security-auditor
frontmatter_yaml.description: Security engineer focused on vulnerability detection, threat modeling, and secure coding practices. Use for security-focused code review, threat analysis, or hardening recommendations.

name: test-engineer
description_raw: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.
description_normalized: QA-engineer persona that designs test suites, writes tests for existing code, and identifies coverage gaps.

frontmatter_yaml.name: test-engineer
frontmatter_yaml.description: QA engineer specialized in test strategy, test writing, and coverage analysis. Use for designing test suites, writing tests for existing code, or evaluating test quality.

name: review
description_raw: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance
description_normalized: Orchestration entry point that invokes code-review-and-quality to evaluate staged or recent changes across five quality axes.

frontmatter_yaml.description: Conduct a five-axis code review — correctness, readability, architecture, security, performance

name: ansible-fleet
description_raw: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g. filtcore for Lars). Use when user says "audit fleet", "audit the machines", "check machine drift", "which machines have X", "add capability to ansible", "add this to the fleet", "remove from fleet", "deprecate capability", "ansible playbook", "fleet config", "group_vars", "host_vars", "fleet alignment", "machine out of sync", "what's on all my machines", "compare machines", "fleet inventory", "provision machine". The skill has three capabilities: (1) Fleet Audit — produce a drift/alignment report across all machines, (2) Capability Lifecycle — add/promote/deprecate a capability with Ansible-compatible classification, (3) Config Variation Management — explain where a setting lives (all/group/host).
description_normalized: Manage and audit the Ansible-provisioned Mac fleet across David's machines (AppyDave group) and client machines (e.g.

name: architectural-review
description_raw: Structural and design quality review of system architecture. Different from code review — this asks "is this system well-structured for growth?" not "is this code correct?". Use when user says "architectural review", "system design review", "is this ready to scale", "structural review", "before adding a major feature", "God class", "too many responsibilities", "design review", "is this maintainable", "refactor planning", "before building on top", "architecture audit", "is this well designed", "responsibility review". Does NOT replace code-quality-audit or test-quality-audit — it adds a third lens on top of them.
description_normalized: Structural and design quality review of system architecture.

name: code-quality-audit
description_raw: Deep code quality audit of production source files. Think like a senior software architect reviewing code before building a major feature on top of it. Use when user says "code quality audit", "audit the code", "review production code", "is this code ready", "grade the code", "quality check", "before building on this", "code health check", "review my implementation", "is this codebase solid". Always launch in parallel with appydave:test-quality-audit — they cover different concerns and neither substitutes for the other.
description_normalized: Deep code quality audit of production source files.

name: codebase-audit
description_raw: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict. Use when user says "codebase audit", "audit the code", "run all three audits", "complete audit", "comprehensive audit", "code review audit", "is this codebase ready". For a layer-aware full system health check use appydave:system-audit instead. For single-lens audits use appydave:code-quality-audit, appydave:test-quality-audit, or appydave:architectural-review directly.
description_normalized: Three-lens codebase audit orchestrator — runs code quality, test quality, and architectural review in parallel as subagents and synthesises a combined readiness verdict.

name: conversation-triage
description_raw: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session. Use when user says "what are the open items", "what did we decide", "what's unresolved", "loose ends", "what did we park", "what are we tracking", "what questions are still open", "triage the conversation", "what have we not finished", "where are we up to", "what did I ask that we never answered".
description_normalized: Mid-conversation audit that surfaces open questions, loose threads, parked topics, decisions made, and unstated assumptions — right now, in the current session.

name: delivery-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches review-blind-hunter, review-edge-case-hunter, review-acceptance-auditor, review-architecture, review-code-quality, and review-unit-tests as background agents, then triages and synthesizes findings into a unified verdict. Use when: 'delivery review', 'review this PR', 'review my changes', 'code review all dimensions', 'full review', 'review before merge', 'is this ready to ship'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel delivery review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review
description_raw: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions. Launches doc-review:coherence, doc-review:gaps, doc-review:completeness, doc-review:topology, doc-review:prose, and doc-review:crossref as background agents, then triages and synthesizes their findings. Use when user mentions: 'review docs', 'doc review', 'review this brain', 'documentation quality', 'full doc audit', 'review all dimensions', 'run doc review'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate parallel documentation review across 6 quality dimensions.

name: doc-review-coherence
description_raw: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CH dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check coherence', 'find contradictions', 'doc contradictions', 'content drift', 'tonal consistency', 'do docs agree'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for coherence — find contradictions between documents, tonal shifts suggesting different authors or time periods, claims that may have drifted from truth, and implicit assumptions that conflict.

name: doc-review-completeness
description_raw: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (CA dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check completeness', 'does it deliver', 'scope check', 'intent vs reality', 'does it cover everything', 'scope gaps', 'scope creep'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for completeness — check whether docs deliver what they promise, map stated intent to actual content, and flag scope gaps and scope creep.

name: doc-review-crossref
description_raw: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (XR dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check cross-references', 'provenance chain', 'examples accurate', 'index sync', 'references current', 'version check', 'source attribution'.
description_normalized: Review documentation cross-reference integrity — verify provenance chains, check that examples match reality, validate index-to-file sync, and confirm version references are current.

name: doc-review-gaps
description_raw: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (GL dimension). Use when user mentions: 'find gaps', 'missing docs', 'dead links', 'orphaned files', 'loose ends', 'what is missing', 'dangling references'.
description_normalized: Review documentation for gaps and loose ends — find missing sections, dead links, orphaned files, dangling references, undefined terms, and promised content that was never written.

name: doc-review-prose
description_raw: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (PU dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check prose', 'terminology consistency', 'formatting check', 'doc style', 'duplicated content', 'DRY docs', 'voice consistency', 'template compliance'.
description_normalized: Review documentation prose and uniformity — check terminology consistency, formatting conventions, voice, DRY (no duplicated content), readability, and template adherence.

name: doc-review-topology
description_raw: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy. Part of the doc-review dimension suite (TS dimension). Use when user mentions: 'check structure', 'file organization', 'doc topology', 'index accuracy', 'navigation', 'directory structure', 'are docs well organized'.
description_normalized: Review documentation topology and structure — check file organization, grouping coherence, separation of concerns, composability, navigation paths, INDEX.md accuracy, and directory taxonomy.

name: false-twins
description_raw: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape. Eight-lens framework plus triangulation. Use when the user says 'false twins', 'differential analysis', 'compare X and Y', 'X vs Y deep dive', 'lookalike audit', 'is this really the same as', 'two systems same problem', 'should I use X or Y', 'what's the actual difference between', 'these look the same — are they?', or when evaluating two competing libraries / SaaS products / methodologies / community platforms / models / vendors that appear interchangeable on the surface.
description_normalized: Differential analysis for two systems, tools, methodologies, products, or platforms that claim to solve the same problem but may differ in mechanism, philosophy, economics, or operational shape.

name: lexi
description_raw: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections). Use when setting up a new brain folder, onboarding a collection of brains, running a full health audit, adding frontmatter, populating INDEX.md, auditing a brain's compliance, syncing file counts, fixing tags, finding duplicate documents, identifying files that need splitting, finding isolated brains, finding orphan/unlinked files, auditing negative-knowledge coverage, adding aliases or tags to a brain, building the machine-readable brains-index.json, or enriching raw intake files. She introduces herself as Lexi. Trigger on phrases like "run lexi", "ask lexi", "set up a new brain", "onboard this brain", "onboard this collection", "initialize this folder", "run audit", "full audit", "audit the brain", "health check the brain", "fix the index", "clean up the brain", "check frontmatter", "add frontmatter", "sync file counts", "fix tags", "tag compliance", "what's missing in the brain", "sweep all brains", "audit the collection", "find duplicates", "search similar", "duplicate check", "find similar documents", "what's duplicated", "find documents to split", "check SRP", "single responsibility", "what needs splitting", "files that cover too much", "split candidates", "find isolated brains", "check isolation", "find orphan files", "find unlinked files", "check orphans", "check happy path", "find brains without anti-patterns", "negative knowledge audit", "add alias", "add tag", "build brains index", "rebuild brain index", "regenerate brains-index", "add brain alias", "add brain tag", "find brain by alias", "enrich raw files", "add frontmatter to intake", "health check raw intake", "normalise transcripts", "enrich transcripts".
description_normalized: Portable brain librarian — curate, onboard, maintain, and analyse second-brain knowledge collections (single brain or multi-brain collections).

name: prompt-injection-scanner
description_raw: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline. Wraps content in an isolated Opus agent for model-pinned, auditable sanitization. Use when: 'scan this for injection', 'sanitize this content', 'check this before processing', 'is this safe to process?', 'scan incoming email / OMI / document / search result', or any time external content (email, web, relay, uploaded files, client documents) is about to be processed by a downstream skill or pipeline.
description_normalized: Scan untrusted content for prompt injection attacks before it enters any pipeline.

name: review-acceptance-auditor
description_raw: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'. Maps ACs to code evidence. Use when: 'check ACs', 'acceptance audit', 'does this meet the spec', 'verify acceptance criteria', 'AC coverage'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AA dimension).
description_normalized: Verify that every acceptance criterion is actually met by the implementation — not just 'code exists' but 'intent is satisfied'.

name: review-architecture
description_raw: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions. Use when: 'architecture review', 'does this follow the architecture', 'check patterns', 'convention compliance', 'structural alignment'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (AR dimension).
description_normalized: Verify code changes align with the project's architecture document — patterns, conventions, boundaries, and technical decisions.

name: review-blind-hunter
description_raw: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight. Cynical, skeptical analysis of code changes — looks for what feels off even when it technically works. Use when: 'blind hunt', 'adversarial review', 'what am I missing', 'find hidden issues', 'cynical review'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (BH dimension).
description_normalized: Adversarial review that finds subtle issues hiding in plain sight.

name: review-code-quality
description_raw: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code. Fast, diff-scoped quality check — not a full audit. Use when: 'code quality review', 'is this clean', 'quality check', 'naming review', 'DRY check'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (CQ dimension).
description_normalized: Assess naming, structure, DRY, separation of concerns, and code craftsmanship in changed code.

name: review-edge-case-hunter
description_raw: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases. Method-driven path enumeration, not intuition. Use when: 'edge cases', 'boundary conditions', 'what could break', 'missing validations', 'path analysis'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (EC dimension).
description_normalized: Exhaustive edge case analysis — walks every branching path and boundary condition, reports only unhandled cases.

name: review-unit-tests
description_raw: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code. Reviews test design, not test execution. Use when: 'test review', 'are tests sufficient', 'test coverage check', 'missing tests', 'test quality'. Part of delivery-review orchestrator (UT dimension).
description_normalized: Evaluate test coverage, test quality, and identify missing test cases for changed code.

name: schedules-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave schedules registry at ~/.config/appydave/schedules.json — the single source of truth for every scheduled job David runs (cron, launchd, Claude Code routines, ScheduleWakeup loops). Use when the user says: "add schedule", "register schedule", "list schedules", "what's running on cron", "what cron jobs do I have", "what's scheduled", "remove schedule", "schedule stats", "where does X run", "schedules on [machine]", "validate schedules", "audit schedules", or "show me my schedules".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave schedules registry at ~/.config/appydave/schedules.json — the single source of truth for every scheduled job David runs (cron, launchd, Claude Code routines, ScheduleWakeup loops).

name: skills-registry
description_raw: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave skills registry at ~/.config/appydave/skills.json — the single source of truth for every Claude Code skill in David's system, with family/kind metadata and detection rules. Use when the user says: "list skills", "what skills do I have", "find skill", "skills by family", "skills by kind", "BMAD skills", "review skills", "import skills", "skill quality scan", "register skill", "deprecate skill", "audit skills", or "show me skills using X tool".
description_normalized: Read, write, and shape the canonical AppyDave skills registry at ~/.config/appydave/skills.json — the single source of truth for every Claude Code skill in David's system, with family/kind metadata and detection rules.

name: system-audit
description_raw: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report. Unlike delivery-review (which is diff-scoped and story-focused), system-audit reads the full codebase and produces a severity-ranked health map. Use when user says: "system audit", "full audit", "codebase health check", "audit the whole system", "audit this codebase", "run a health check", "full system review", "review the whole codebase", "audit before onboarding", "what's wrong with this codebase". Supported stacks: Next.js App Router, RVETS (AppyStack), Baku (claude.ai/claude-ship), Astro, Rails, Ruby gem, AppySentinel. Accepts a manual layer manifest for any other stack.
description_normalized: Full codebase health audit orchestrator — auto-detects the tech stack, maps the codebase into architectural layers, assigns the right specialist mix per layer, launches all layer reviews in parallel as background agents, then synthesises findings into a unified SYSTEM-HEALTH.md report.

name: test-quality-audit
description_raw: Deep test quality audit of the test suite. Think like a senior test engineer reviewing whether tests actually catch real regressions. Use when user says "test quality audit", "audit the tests", "check test suite", "are my tests good", "would tests catch regressions", "test health check", "review test coverage", "test quality check", "grade the tests", "test suite review". Always launch in parallel with appydave:code-quality-audit — they cover different concerns and neither substitutes for the other.
description_normalized: Deep test quality audit of the test suite.

name: ux-review
description_raw: Orchestrate multi-tier UX review across 14 design quality dimensions. Launches Impeccable system skills (frontend-design, arrange, typeset, colorize, clarify, onboard, critique, bolder, quieter, distill, delight, adapt, harden, optimize, polish) in 5 tiered waves, then triages and synthesizes into a unified UX quality report. Use when user mentions: 'ux review', 'review the UI', 'design review', 'review this component', 'full ux audit', 'check the design', 'design quality check', 'review the frontend'.
description_normalized: Orchestrate multi-tier UX review across 14 design quality dimensions.

name: security-analyst
description_raw: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment. Use when user mentions: "security", "vulnerability", "is this secure", "threat model", "authentication review", "authorization", "SQL injection", "XSS", "OWASP", "sensitive data", "harden", "security audit", "exploit", "CVE"
description_normalized: Security engineer specializing in application security, threat modeling, and vulnerability assessment.

name: bmad-code-review
description_raw: 'Review code changes adversarially using parallel review layers (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor) with structured triage into actionable categories. Use when the user says "run code review" or "review this code"'
description_normalized: 'Review code changes adversarially using parallel review layers (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor) with structured triage into actionable categories.

frontmatter_yaml.name: bmad-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: 'Review code changes adversarially using parallel review layers (Blind Hunter, Edge Case Hunter, Acceptance Auditor) with structured triage into actionable categories. Use when the user says "run code review" or "review this code"'

name: bmad-editorial-review-prose
description_raw: 'Clinical copy-editor that reviews text for communication issues. Use when user says review for prose or improve the prose'
description_normalized: 'Clinical copy-editor that reviews text for communication issues.

frontmatter_yaml.name: bmad-editorial-review-prose
frontmatter_yaml.description: 'Clinical copy-editor that reviews text for communication issues. Use when user says review for prose or improve the prose'

name: bmad-editorial-review-structure
description_raw: 'Structural editor that proposes cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension. Use when user requests structural review or editorial review of structure'
description_normalized: 'Structural editor that proposes cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension.

frontmatter_yaml.name: bmad-editorial-review-structure
frontmatter_yaml.description: 'Structural editor that proposes cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension. Use when user requests structural review or editorial review of structure'

name: bmad-review-adversarial-general
description_raw: 'Perform a Cynical Review and produce a findings report. Use when the user requests a critical review of something'
description_normalized: 'Perform a Cynical Review and produce a findings report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: bmad-review-adversarial-general
frontmatter_yaml.description: 'Perform a Cynical Review and produce a findings report. Use when the user requests a critical review of something'

name: bmad-review-edge-case-hunter
description_raw: 'Walk every branching path and boundary condition in content, report only unhandled edge cases. Orthogonal to adversarial review - method-driven not attitude-driven. Use when you need exhaustive edge-case analysis of code, specs, or diffs.'
description_normalized: 'Walk every branching path and boundary condition in content, report only unhandled edge cases.

frontmatter_yaml.name: bmad-review-edge-case-hunter
frontmatter_yaml.description: 'Walk every branching path and boundary condition in content, report only unhandled edge cases. Orthogonal to adversarial review - method-driven not attitude-driven. Use when you need exhaustive edge-case analysis of code, specs, or diffs.'

name: ce-agent-native-audit
description_raw: Run comprehensive agent-native architecture review with scored principles
description_normalized: Runs a parallel subagent audit of agent-native architecture principles and produces a scored report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-audit
frontmatter_yaml.description: Run comprehensive agent-native architecture review with scored principles

name: ce-code-review
description_raw: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
description_normalized: Orchestrates tiered parallel reviewer agents that return structured JSON findings, deduplicates, gates by confidence, and assembles a single code review report.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-code-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Structured code review using tiered persona agents, confidence-gated findings, and a merge/dedup pipeline. Use when reviewing code changes before creating a PR.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [blank to review current branch, or provide PR link]

name: ce-doc-review
description_raw: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues. Use when a requirements document or plan document exists and the user wants to improve it.
description_normalized: Dispatches specialized document-reviewer agents in parallel, auto-applies safe fixes, and routes remaining findings through a four-option interaction for user decision.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-doc-review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Review requirements or plan documents using parallel persona agents that surface role-specific issues.

name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
description_raw: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel. Use when addressing PR review comments, resolving review threads, or fixing code review feedback.
description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, spawns parallel resolver agents per thread, implements fixes, replies, and resolves threads.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-resolve-pr-feedback
frontmatter_yaml.description: Resolve PR review feedback by evaluating validity and fixing issues in parallel.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [PR number, comment URL, or blank for current branch's PR]

name: ce-simplify-code
description_raw: Simplify and refine recently changed code for clarity, reuse, quality, and efficiency while preserving behavior.
description_normalized: Reviews and simplifies recently changed code for clarity, consistency, and maintainability while preserving exact functionality, then verifies via the test suite.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-simplify-code
frontmatter_yaml.description: Simplify and refine recently changed code for clarity, reuse, quality, and efficiency while preserving behavior.
frontmatter_yaml.argument-hint: [blank to simplify current branch changes, or describe what to simplify]

name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents -- those with significant architectural decisions, new abstractions, or more than 5 requirements. Challenges premises, surfaces unstated assumptions, and stress-tests decisions rather than evaluating document quality.
description_normalized: Conditionally applied document-review agent that challenges plan premises and stress-tests decisions via a 5-technique falsification protocol.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-document-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional document-review persona for high-stakes documents.

name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff is large (>=50 changed lines) or touches high-risk domains like auth, payments, data mutations, or external APIs. Actively constructs failure scenarios to break the implementation rather than checking against known patterns.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that constructs specific failure scenarios to attack implementations in large or high-risk diffs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-adversarial-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for large or high-risk diffs.

name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity -- any action a user can take, an agent can also take. Use after adding UI features, agent tools, or system prompts.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that finds gaps where users can do things agents cannot, enforcing action parity and dynamic context injection.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-agent-native-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews code to ensure agent-native parity.

name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches API routes, request/response types, serialization, versioning, or exported type signatures. Reviews code for breaking contract changes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that hunts for breaking API contract changes — removed endpoints, changed response shapes, missing versioning, inconsistent error shapes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-api-contract-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for API route and contract changes.

name: ce-architecture-strategist
description_raw: Analyzes code changes from an architectural perspective for pattern compliance and design integrity. Use when reviewing PRs, adding services, or evaluating structural refactors.
description_normalized: Evaluates code changes for architectural pattern compliance, coupling, cohesion, and long-term system implications.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-architecture-strategist
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code changes from an architectural perspective.

name: ce-code-simplicity-reviewer
description_raw: Final review pass to ensure code is as simple and minimal as possible. Use after implementation is complete to identify YAGNI violations and simplification opportunities.
description_normalized: Final-pass code-review agent that ruthlessly simplifies code via YAGNI principle, removing unnecessary abstraction and redundancy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-code-simplicity-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Final review pass to ensure code is as simple and minimal as possible.

name: ce-coherence-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency -- contradictions between sections, terminology drift, structural issues, and ambiguity where readers would diverge. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that catches internal contradictions, terminology drift, and structural inconsistencies within requirements or plan documents.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-coherence-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for internal consistency.

name: ce-correctness-reviewer
description_raw: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs, error propagation failures, and intent-vs-implementation mismatches.
description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that mentally executes code to catch logic errors, null propagation, race conditions, and broken error propagation.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-correctness-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona. Reviews code for logic errors, edge cases, state management bugs.

name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
description_raw: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety. Use when checking migration safety, data constraints, transaction boundaries, or privacy compliance.
description_normalized: Specialist agent that audits database migrations and data models for reversibility, NULL handling, ACID compliance, and privacy regulation requirements.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-integrity-guardian
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews database migrations, data models, and persistent data code for safety.

name: ce-data-migration-expert
description_raw: Validates data migrations, backfills, and production data transformations against reality. Use when PRs involve ID mappings, column renames, enum conversions, or schema changes.
description_normalized: Validates that data migrations match production reality, catches swapped mappings, and ensures concrete SQL verification plans and rollback safety exist.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migration-expert
frontmatter_yaml.description: Validates data migrations and backfills against production reality.

name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches migration files, schema changes, data transformations, or backfill scripts. Reviews code for data integrity and migration safety.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent specializing in migration safety — swapped mappings, irreversible changes, missing backfills, and schema drift during deploy windows.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-data-migrations-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for migration files and schema changes.

name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
description_raw: Visually compares live UI implementation against Figma designs and provides detailed feedback on discrepancies. Use after writing or modifying HTML/CSS/React components to verify design fidelity.
description_normalized: Captures screenshots of live UI and compares against Figma designs, producing actionable discrepancy feedback on spacing, typography, color, and responsiveness.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-implementation-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Visually compares live UI against Figma designs.

name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
description_raw: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions -- information architecture, interaction states, user flows, and AI slop risk. Uses dimensional rating to identify gaps. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that identifies missing design decisions in plans using a dimensional 0-10 rating system, flagging only scores below 7.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-design-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for missing design decisions.

name: ce-dhh-rails-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when Rails diffs introduce architectural choices, abstractions, or frontend patterns that may fight the framework. Reviews code from an opinionated DHH perspective.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that reviews Rails code from DHH's perspective, catching abstractions, JS-world patterns, and convention violations that fight the framework.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-dhh-rails-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona reviewing Rails from DHH's perspective.

name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
description_raw: Evaluates whether proposed technical approaches in planning documents will survive contact with reality -- architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, migration risks, and implementability. Spawned by the document-review skill.
description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates whether a plan's technical approach is actually buildable, checking architecture conflicts, dependency gaps, and environmental assumptions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-feasibility-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates technical feasibility of planning documents.

name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches async UI code, Stimulus/Turbo lifecycles, or DOM-timing-sensitive frontend behavior. Reviews code for race conditions and janky UI failure modes.
description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches frontend race conditions — lifecycle cleanup gaps, Turbo/Stimulus timing bugs, concurrent interaction conflicts, and stale async work.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-julik-frontend-races-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for async UI and DOM-timing issues.

name: ce-kieran-python-reviewer
description_raw: Conditional code-review persona, selected when the diff touches Python code. Reviews changes with Kieran's strict bar for Pythonic clarity, type hints, and maintainability.
description_normalized: Conditional Python code-review agent enforcing Pythonic clarity, type hints, testable structure, and explicit error handling.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-kieran-python-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for Python diffs.

description_normalized: Conditional Rails code-review agent that enforces clarity, conventions, and maintainability, prioritizing the next reader six months from now.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-kieran-rails-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for Rails diffs.

description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that reads from the perspective of the next developer six months from now, catching premature abstraction, dead code, and coupling.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-maintainability-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona for maintainability issues.

name: ce-pattern-recognition-specialist
description_raw: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, naming conventions, and duplication. Use when checking codebase consistency or verifying new code follows established patterns.
description_normalized: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, technical debt markers, and consistency with established conventions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-pattern-recognition-specialist
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code for design patterns, anti-patterns, and naming conventions.

description_normalized: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks across algorithmic complexity, database queries, memory allocation, caching, and scalability dimensions.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-oracle
frontmatter_yaml.description: Analyzes code for performance bottlenecks and scalability issues.

description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that catches N+1 queries, unbounded memory growth, missing pagination, hot-path allocations, and blocking I/O in async contexts.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-performance-reviewer

description_normalized: Evaluates PR review threads for validity, implements fixes, and returns structured summaries with reply text; supports standard and cluster modes.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-pr-comment-resolver
frontmatter_yaml.description: Evaluates and resolves PR review threads.

description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that verifies prior review feedback has been addressed, serving as institutional memory across the PR review cycle.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-previous-comments-reviewer

description_normalized: Document-review agent that challenges premises and assesses strategic consequences of requirements or plans from a senior product leader perspective.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-product-lens-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents as a senior product leader.

description_normalized: Always-on code-review agent that audits changes against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md standards files, citing specific rules for every finding.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-project-standards-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Always-on code-review persona auditing against CLAUDE.md and AGENTS.md.

description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that traces failure propagation paths, catching missing error handling, unbounded retries, missing timeouts, and cascading failure patterns.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-reliability-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Conditional code-review persona for error handling and reliability code.

description_normalized: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes by cross-referencing included migrations, preventing accidental inclusion of changes from other branches.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-schema-drift-detector
frontmatter_yaml.description: Detects unrelated schema.rb changes in PRs.

description_normalized: Document-review agent that asks whether plans are right-sized for their goals and whether every abstraction earns its keep.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-scope-guardian-reviewer
frontmatter_yaml.description: Reviews planning documents for scope alignment and unjustified complexity.

description_normalized: Document-review agent that evaluates security gaps in plans before implementation — threat model completeness, auth/authz commitments, and secret handling strategy.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-lens-reviewer

description_normalized: Conditional code-review agent that thinks like an attacker, tracing injection vectors, auth bypasses, secrets in code, insecure deserialization, and SSRF paths.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-reviewer

description_normalized: Performs comprehensive security audits covering input validation, auth/authz, hardcoded secrets, and OWASP compliance using systematic scan protocols.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-security-sentinel

Reviews for SwiftUI correctness, state management, memory safety, Swift concurrency, Core Data threading, and accessibility.

frontmatter_yaml.name: ce-swift-ios-reviewer

name: kw:review
description_raw: Multi-reviewer quality check for knowledge work. Runs strategic alignment and data accuracy reviewers on plans, briefs, and strategy docs.

frontmatter_yaml.name: kw:review
frontmatter_yaml.description: Multi-reviewer quality check for knowledge work. Runs strategic alignment and data accuracy reviewers on plans, briefs, and strategy docs.

name: strategic-alignment-reviewer
description_raw: Use this agent when you need to review knowledge work artifacts (plans, briefs, strategy docs, campaign proposals) for strategic alignment. This includes checking whether goals are explicit, hypotheses are falsifiable, success metrics are measurable, scope is proportional to impact, and the work serves stated objectives.
description_normalized: Reviews plans and briefs for strategic soundness across 7 criteria (goal clarity, falsifiability, metrics, scope, resources, consistency, opportunity cost), returning P1/P2/P3 findings grouped by severity.

name: data-accuracy-reviewer
description_raw: Use this agent when you need to verify that numbers, metrics, and data claims in knowledge work artifacts are accurate, sourced, and fresh. This includes checking that every number has a cited source, comparison baselines are explicit, data matches canonical definitions, and known caveats are acknowledged.
description_normalized: Verifies every data claim in a knowledge work artifact against 7 criteria (source citation, baselines, canonical definitions, freshness, caveats, hardcoded vs live, baseline appropriateness), returning P1/P2/P3 findings.

name: accessibility

name: design-review
description_raw:
Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,
AI slop patterns, and slow interactions — then fixes them. Iteratively fixes issues
in source code, committing each fix atomically and re-verifying with before/after
screenshots. For plan-mode design review (before implementation), use /plan-design-review.
Use when asked to "audit the design", "visual QA", "check if it looks good", or "design polish".
Proactively suggest when the user mentions visual inconsistencies or
wants to polish the look of a live site. (gstack)
description_normalized: Designer's eye QA: finds visual inconsistency, spacing issues, hierarchy problems,

name: health
description_raw:
Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,
test runner, dead code detector, shell linter), computes a weighted composite
0-10 score, and tracks trends over time. Use when: "health check",
"code quality", "how healthy is the codebase", "run all checks",
"quality score". (gstack)
description_normalized: Code quality dashboard. Wraps existing project tools (type checker, linter,

name: plan-design-review
description_raw:
Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.
Rates each design dimension 0-10, explains what would make it a 10,
then fixes the plan to get there. Works in plan mode. For live site
visual audits, use /design-review. Use when asked to "review the design plan"
or "design critique".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan with UI/UX components that
should be reviewed before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Designer's eye plan review — interactive, like CEO and Eng review.

name: plan-devex-review
description_raw:
Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,
benchmarks against competitors, designs magical moments, and traces friction
points before scoring. Three modes: DX EXPANSION (competitive advantage),
DX POLISH (bulletproof every touchpoint), DX TRIAGE (critical gaps only).
Use when asked to "DX review", "developer experience audit", "devex review",
or "API design review".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan for developer-facing products
(APIs, CLIs, SDKs, libraries, platforms, docs). (gstack)
description_normalized: Interactive developer experience plan review. Explores developer personas,

name: plan-eng-review
description_raw:
Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,
data flow, diagrams, edge cases, test coverage, performance. Walks through
issues interactively with opinionated recommendations. Use when asked to
"review the architecture", "engineering review", or "lock in the plan".
Proactively suggest when the user has a plan or design doc and is about to
start coding — to catch architecture issues before implementation. (gstack)
description_normalized: Eng manager-mode plan review. Lock in the execution plan — architecture,

name: review
description_raw:
Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust
boundary violations, conditional side effects, and other structural issues. Use when
asked to "review this PR", "code review", "pre-landing review", or "check my diff".
Proactively suggest when the user is about to merge or land code changes. (gstack)
description_normalized: Pre-landing PR review. Analyzes diff against the base branch for SQL safety, LLM trust

description_raw: Claims-based authorization for agents and operations. Grant, revoke, and verify permissions for secure multi-agent coordination. Use when: permission management, access control, secure operations, authorization checks. Skip when: open access, no security requirements, single-agent local work.
description_normalized: Claims-based authorization for agents and operations.

name: Pair Programming
description_raw: AI-assisted pair programming with multiple modes (driver$navigator$switch), real-time verification, quality monitoring, and comprehensive testing. Supports TDD, debugging, refactoring, and learning sessions. Features automatic role switching, continuous code review, security scanning, and performance optimization with truth-score verification.
description_normalized: AI-assisted pair programming with multiple modes (driver$navigator$switch), real-time verification, quality monitoring, and comprehensive testing.

name: security-audit

Use when: authentication implementation, authorization logic, payment processing, user data handling, API endpoint creation, file upload handling, database queries, external API integration. Skip when: read-only operations on publi

Skip when: single fil
description_normalized: Multi-agent swarm coordination for complex tasks.

name: receiving-code-review
description_raw: Use when receiving code review feedback, before implementing suggestions, especially if feedback seems unclear or technically questionable - requires technical rigor and verification, not performative agreement or blind implementation. Enforces: read, understand, verify, evaluate, respond technically, implement one item at a time.
description_normalized: Code review reception skill that enforces technical verification over performative agreement before implementing any feedback.

frontmatter_yaml.name: receiving-code-review

name: requesting-code-review
description_raw: Use when completing tasks, implementing major features, or before merging to verify work meets requirements. Dispatches a code reviewer subagent with precisely crafted context (git SHAs, description, requirements) to catch issues before they cascade. Mandatory after each task in subagent-driven development.
description_normalized: Code review request skill that dispatches a focused reviewer subagent with crafted context after each implementation task.
